pr.title: Fix Mooncake dummy client to include hybrid (Mamba) buffers
pr.body: ### What - In Mooncake standalone (dummy client) mode, compute the `setup_dummy()` shared mapping size from **all host buffers** that may be passed to `register_buffer()` (KV anchor + hybrid pools like Mamba temporal/conv). - Register the regression test in CPU CI. - Refactor the regression test to follow `.claude/skills/write-sglang-test` conventions. ### Why - Hybrid Mamba models register additional host buffers after initialization (`register_mem_host_pool_v2`). In dummy mode the real `mooncake_client` must be able to map these buffers; previously `setup_dummy()` only sized the mapping based on KV, so Mamba buffers were not guaranteed to be visible. ### Changes - `python/sglang/srt/mem_cache/storage/mooncake_store/mooncake_store.py` - Add `_standalone_required_bytes()` and use it for `setup_dummy()`. - `test/registered/unit/mem_cache/test_mooncake_standalone_dummy_mamba.py` - Unit test asserting `setup_dummy(required_bytes, ...)` includes hybrid buffers. - Registered with `register_cpu_ci(est_time=1, suite="base-a-test-cpu")`. - Uses `CustomTestCase`, `unittest.main()`, and `patch.dict` for fake module injection. ### Notes - No behavior change for non-standalone mode.
pr.author: stmatengss
